missing_info: I don't have evidence that the destination host 172.16.0.11 is up or reachable on the R2 LAN. Please provide: from R2 -> show ip arp 172.16.0.11 and show interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0 (status/line-protocol).
I need to confirm routing between R1 and R2 for the 172.16.0.0/24 network. Please provide: show ip route on R1 and show ip route on R2.
I need active connectivity tests to locate where packets are dropped. Please run and provide results of: from R1: ping 172.16.0.11 and traceroute 172.16.0.11; from R2: ping 172.16.0.11.

Confirm whether any host-level firewall or host down condition exists for 172.16.0.11 (e.g., is the host powered / firewall enabled?). If possible, provide the host OS firewall status or a test ping from another device on the 172.16.0.0/24 LAN.
From the endpoint: paste the output of ping (preferably with 25 packets) and traceroute from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 (include full output and any error messages).
On R1 and R2: provide current access-list counters — output of 'show ip access-lists' (or 'show access-lists') so we can see hit counts for R1_IN, R1_TO_R2 and R2_TO_R1.
On R1: provide connectivity and host-facing info: 'show ip arp 192.168.0.11' and 'show interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0' (to confirm the host is on-link and the interface state).

plan_history.intent_description: 172.16.0.11 is unreachable from 192.168.0.11 (0/25 pings). Impact: traffic from source cannot reach host on 172.16.0.0/24. Desired outcome: locate where packets are dropped and restore end-to-end reachability (repair routing/interface/host as needed).
plan_history.problem: Host 172.16.0.11 is not responding to pings from 192.168.0.11 (0/25). Unknown whether issue is host down/firewall, missing ARP, interface down on R2, or missing/incorrect routing between R1 and R2.
plan_history.fix_summary: Gather ARP/interface/routing evidence from R1 and R2 and run active connectivity tests (ping/traceroute) from both routers to locate failure point. After identifying cause, apply corrective action (e.g., restore interface, add route, clear ARP, or remediate host firewall).

plan_history.intent_description: 192.168.0.11 cannot reach 172.16.0.11 (0/25 pings). Impact: traffic from 192.168.0.0/24 cannot reach 172.16.0.0/24. Desired outcome: locate packet drop between 192.168.0.11 and the network and restore end-to-end reachability.
plan_history.problem: Host 192.168.0.11 cannot reach 172.16.0.11 (0/25 pings). Routers can reach 172.16.0.11; packets are likely dropped between 192.168.0.11 and router1 (possible R1 ACL, ARP, or interface issue).
plan_history.fix_summary: Collect R1 diagnostics (running-config and Gi0/0/0 status) and test reachability from R1 to the endpoint. Based on results: remove/modify any R1 ACL blocking traffic from 192.168.0.0/24 to 172.16.0.0/24 or repair R1 interface/ARP or host networking to restore reachability.

remaining_issues: No direct verification that host 192.168.0.11 can reach 172.16.0.11 (the original reported problem). The tool outputs only show R1 can reach both endpoints, not host-to-host connectivity.

Traceroute from R1 to 192.168.0.11 returned no hops (all '*'), which is inconsistent with the successful ping and should be investigated (could indicate devices not responding to TTL probes or an intermediate device filtering traceroute probes).

R1 has an ACL 'R1_IN' that denies ICMP with source 172.16.0.0/24 applied inbound on the Serial0/1/0 interface — this ACL could affect return traffic for host-to-host pings (needs hit-count verification).
We do not have access-list hit counters to confirm whether R1 or R2 ACLs are actually dropping relevant packets.
We do not have ARP or host-facing interface details on R1 for 192.168.0.11 to confirm the host is on-link and ARP/resolution is correct.
